behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because the failure is at a component handoff boundary: unit-floor evidence is "content passed to the summary-generation step" plus "data retrieved from all three sources" (collector/aggregator -> summarizer contract). I rejected Unit because a single-function test can miss cross-module wiring bugs (missing source in assembled payload). I rejected System because the behavior text does not require real network/OS effects; it only checks assembled handoff content. I rejected Agentic/Workflow because this behavior does not evaluate model judgment or multi-step agent decisions; it stops at pre-generation input composition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector-orchestration modules with in-process fakes for metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log clients. Return distinct sentinel fields from each fake, run the collection->payload assembly path, and assert the exact payload passed into summary generation contains all three source sections and expected key fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because this is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering: unit-floor evidence is "one data source is unavailable" combined with "report is produced with a `data unavailable` note" (source client failure -> report assembler output). I rejected Unit because local logic tests can miss exception mapping between modules. I rejected System: the behavior does not require proving real network outage semantics, only correct fallback behavior when a source fails. I rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here is failure handling and section labeling, not model output quality or chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline in-process with three source adapters where one adapter raises an availability error and two return normal data. Assert the pipeline completes, does not abort, and emits a report containing a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section while preserving other sections.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Agentic is the minimum because correctness depends on real model output quality: "narrative summary accurately represents the key values" is about generation fidelity, not just transport/orchestration. A stubbed summarizer would always pass and hide hallucination/omission errors (deterministic-assertion trap). I rejected Integration because it can verify routing/retries/payload shape but not whether the model actually preserves key facts. I rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single generation judgment, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and evaluate whether produced summaries preserve required facts (values, trends, and caveats) with a rubric-based checker. Run multiple trials per fixture to detect non-deterministic drift and fail if factual coverage/accuracy drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: System is the minimum because the failure boundary includes real external infrastructure: "from data collection through email delivery" and "reaches the configured recipient's inbox" require real HTTP/API + mail transport behavior that in-process fakes can hide (auth, SMTP/API acceptance vs actual inbox delivery, formatting/encoding issues). I rejected Integration because it cannot prove real inbox receipt. I rejected Agentic because this behavior’s assertion is delivery success, not model judgment quality. I rejected Workflow because it does not require multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report job against live/staging source endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify the message is delivered to configured recipients, appears in inbox, and contains expected report sections and metadata (subject/date/recipient headers) end-to-end.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: System is the minimum because the behavior depends on real infrastructure boundaries: "email delivery service is unreachable" plus "saved to a local file" and "retried on the next scheduled run" involve network failure semantics, filesystem persistence, and scheduler/runtime behavior. In-process mocks can hide real failures (path permissions, atomic writes, process restart/state carryover, actual retry trigger timing). I rejected Integration because it can validate fallback logic but not these OS/runtime failure modes. I rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here is transport/persistence/retry mechanics, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: In a test environment, make the real email endpoint unreachable (or blackhole route), run the scheduled job, assert a report artifact is persisted on disk, then restore email reachability and trigger/await the next scheduled run; assert the persisted report is retried and successfully delivered, and local retry state is cleared/updated correctly.
